tactical.rationale: RSI 51.8 is dead neutral, price +0.99% today sitting right on the 50-day SMA ($12.18) and comfortably above the 200-day ($11.57) — a textbook golden-cross posture but with no momentum thrust. Freight rates matter more than price technicals here: Drewry WCI has been drifting lower through mid-2026 as Red Sea rerouting normalizes and new-build capacity from the 2021-22 order boom keeps hitting the water. Spot rates on Asia-Europe/trans-Pacific are well off the 2024 Red Sea spikes. No tactical edge either direction; a whisper of trim bias given rate softness not yet in the tape.

positional.rationale: GSCPI at 1.25 is elevated and the engine reads it as a freight tailwind, but I discount this: much of the 2024-25 supply-chain pressure came from Red Sea rerouting that inflated ton-miles and rates artificially. As those normalize and record newbuild deliveries absorb capacity, the tailwind is fading, not building. Price above both moving averages is real but the 43-day HOLD streak and z=+0.92 tell me nothing structurally new. I nudge slightly below the quant's +8 toward mid-teen positive — modest trim bias into rate normalization and capacity oversupply. P/B 0.71 (below book) is a genuine floor that caps downside.
det_notes: GSCPI 1.25: elevated pressure — freight revenue tailwind

strategic.rationale: This is where the cyclical value case lives. P/B 0.71 is below book — below replacement value for the fleet — a classic Peter Lynch cyclical buy marker. P/E 21x is mid-cycle (not trough, not peak) so no strong inverted-PE signal. The sum-of-parts is compelling: the market is again pricing the Logistics & Services segment near zero during the freight down-leg, while pure-play peers DSV (~20x EV/EBITDA) and Kuehne+Nagel (~15-20x) command premium multiples. Maersk's aggressive buyback (shrinking share count below book) is accretive per-share. HY OAS at 267bps tight = healthy trade/credit backdrop. I align close to the quant's -11 but push slightly more constructive given the SOTP undervaluation and below-replacement fleet value that survive even a soft freight cycle.
det_notes: AMKBY P/B 0.71: below book — value territory
HY OAS 2.67bps: tight — healthy trade environment
GSCPI 1.25: above average — healthy freight demand

composite.summary: Quant composite sits near zero; my blend leans marginally constructive on the strength of below-book/below-replacement P/B (0.71), a Logistics segment priced near zero vs DSV/K+N peers, and ongoing buybacks — offset by a fading GSCPI/freight tailwind as Red Sea rerouting normalizes and newbuild capacity floods in. Net: a low-conviction HOLD with a slight value tilt.

tactical.bull_case: A fresh Suez/Red Sea escalation or a tariff-front-loading demand surge could re-spike spot rates within days, and with RSI only at 51 there is ample room to run before overbought — the golden cross would then confirm a breakout.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day ($11.57) on elevated volume would flip this to a clear sell (positive) tactical signal; a >10% single-week WCI spike from geopolitical shock would flip it to a buy (negative).

positional.bear_case: Container capacity glut from 2021-22 orderbook + Red Sea normalization drives spot rates down through H2 2026; Maersk's Ocean segment margins compress and the stock rerates lower despite the golden cross — GSCPI 'tailwind' proves to be a lagging mirage.
positional.bull_case: Tariff-driven front-loading of US imports plus persistent Red Sea diversion keeps ton-miles elevated and the freight market tighter than feared; buybacks (Maersk has been returning capital aggressively) plus 3% yield and sub-book P/B create a floor with upside if rates hold.
positional.falsifier: WCI/SCFI stabilizing or re-accelerating for 3+ consecutive weeks alongside GSCPI staying >1.5 would flip this to a buy (negative) signal; conversely a decisive rate collapse below cash-breakeven confirms the trim.

strategic.bear_case: Structural container overcapacity means shipping ROICs stay below cost of capital for years; below-book valuation is 'cheap for a reason' — a capital-destroying industry where the Gemini alliance transition adds execution risk and the Logistics segment keeps disappointing vs DSV, so the SOTP discount never closes.
strategic.bull_case: Below-replacement P/B + buybacks shrinking the float + a still-healthy credit environment + eventual Logistics rerating toward peers = multi-year value realization; if freight normalizes at a higher structural floor (Red Sea permanence, decarb-driven slow steaming), through-cycle earnings power is understated.

Trailing P/E 49.7x is at the LOW end of ISRG's decade-long 50-80x range and cohort premium of +88.9% sits comfortably within the 60-120% structural baseline (below mid-premium = buy-leaning) — this is a franchise trading toward the cheap end of its own history and its cohort. The strategic thesis: two-decade moat (11,400 installed base, surgeon training, 40M-procedure data) being PROBED (expected state — score neutral, not eroding) by Hugo/Ottava while the 2027 transition remains unquantified fear. Moat status = probing (neutral), transition status = unquantified_fear (no relief, no penalty). The engine's -12 under-weights how far the multiple has compressed relative to a business with no confirmed erosion. I go -34: a real strategic buy on a best-in-class moat compounder at the low end of its historical multiple with the derate driven by bounded, unquantified fear rather than realized share loss.
